discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day 8 of steady state. Both sites remain unindexed by search engines — confirmed this run via direct site:john09289.github.io / site:funwithscience-org.github.io queries returning zero results. Dome machine-readable layer unchanged: ai_manifest.json V51.1 (last_updated 2026-04-08, confirmed 69, registry_accuracy_pct 94.5), llms.txt (ECM crawl guide, WIN-069 Australia road scaffold + WIN-068 eclipse ensemble as strongest steelman), sitemap.xml (22 URLs, all lastmod 2026-04-08). Our side: llms.txt (accurate vs current data), sitemap.xml (2 URLs), robots.txt, ClaimReview JSON-LD (accurate, matches dome's 94.5%/69-claim claim). No rebrand signals. Indexing strategy (SOCIAL-NOTE-002 deliverable, monitor/social/drafts/indexing-strategy.md) remains the critical path awaiting human execution on Google Search Console + Bing Webmaster Tools verification.

human_decisions_needed.question: Execute search engine indexing plan (items 1-4 from monitor/social/drafts/indexing-strategy.md)
human_decisions_needed.context: Standing reminder, day 4 since last flagged. The indexing strategy was delivered 2026-04-11. Google Search Console verification, Bing Webmaster Tools sitemap submission, and GitHub repo description/topics updates all require human account access. Total time: ~35 minutes. Nothing else in this pipeline moves the discoverability needle until at least one search engine indexes us. Counterargument (per user preference): the dome site is equally unindexed, so the search vacuum is symmetric — there is no imminent loss. However, being first-to-index still provides asymmetric advantage if the dome ever breaks through, so executing now remains the correct move. Also worth noting: our GitHub repo description/topics are a zero-cost low-risk step that doesn't even require Search Console signup.

search_monitoring.trend_summary: Day 8 of monitoring. Both sites remain completely absent from all search engine indices. New observation this run: searching 'aetheric refraction' surfaces legitimate mainstream Einstein-aether / axionic-aether academic work (arXiv, MDPI Universe journal). This is notable because it means the ECM's vocabulary partially collides with real physics vocabulary — a neutral observation about discoverability, not a claim about ECM's scientific merit. It does mean search queries for 'aether' variants will tend to surface academic aether field theory before ever surfacing either side of the dome debate. Together with the 'ECM=automotive' collision and the firmament-space saturation by ANIMA Framework / Journal of Geocentric Cosmology, the dome's chosen vocabulary is in a crowded semantic space for search engines — partially why the dome's raw text search footprint is so small.

strategic_notes: Day 8 quiet steady state. Dome's automated commit pipeline ticks along without human intervention; human-editable machine-readable files have been frozen for a full week. Our machine-readable layer is accurate and consistent. Only new finding this run: the 'aetheric' vocabulary partially collides with legitimate Einstein-aether / axionic-aether academic search space — a neutral discoverability observation rather than a new claim overlap, since these academic papers share zero mathematical or structural content with ECM. Combined with the prior observations (ECM=automotive, firmament space saturated by ANIMA/JGC), the dome sits in a semantically crowded search neighborhood, which is a headwind for both sides' natural discoverability. The #1 actionable blocker remains search engine indexing setup (human action required per SOCIAL-NOTE-002 deliverable). Counterargument retained per user preference: symmetric vacuum means no imminent loss; asymmetric-advantage case remains the reason to act now. No new drafts needed this run. No issues to file.
